The Company supplies energy and related services to Commercial & Industrial customers as well as several private corporate houses, operating both as an Independent Power Producer (IPP) and a Captive Power Producer (CPP). As an IPP, we reliably supply renewable power to esteemed corporate houses through Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs). As a CPP, we provide our corporate customers with the opportunity to own solar and hybrid power projects tailored to their specific requirements.
The leadership team conducts meetings with the customers and other value chain partners periodically.

The company is committed to leveraging the growing opportunities in the renewable energy sector to deliver Clean and Green Power, thereby contributing to societal well-being and environmental sustainability and supporting India’s national goal of Generating 500 GW of non-fossil fuel-based energy capacity by 2030.
Failure to comply with emission norms could lead to a negative/ inevitable long-term impact on the environment, society and may also lead to significant reputational damage.
The company has an effective monitoring mechanism in place for emission management, actively supporting India’s commitment to achieving net zero emissions by 2070.

The Company is committed to delivering high-quality solar and wind-solar hybrid energy infrastructure at competitive prices within committed timelines. With a strong customer-centric approach, we strive to build an ethically sound, sustainable, and stakeholder-friendly corporate entity.

Power generation operations involve significant health and safety risks, including those associated with operating heavy machinery, electrical hazards, and site-related incidents for employees and workers at solar and hybrid power plants. Non-compliance with health and safety standards could also lead to monetary penalties and reputational impact.

The company has a Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Policy and is committed to conduct all activities in a manner that eliminates hazards and minimizes risks to employees and assets, while fully complying with applicable health and safety regulations. It fosters a culture of continuous learning and strict adherence to HSE systems and procedures among all employees and contractors.

The process of project development in solar and hybrid energy entails various risks, including securing statutory approvals and land use clearances, acquiring suitable land, obtaining grid connectivity approvals, and addressing Right-of-Way (RoW) challenges for transmission infrastructure. These uncertainties can lead to project delays, cancellations, or write-offs, which may significantly impact the Company’s financial performance.

The Company proactively addresses project development risks through robust strategies aimed at minimizing their potential impact. This includes conducting comprehensive feasibility studies, strengthening project planning and execution capabilities, optimizing project timelines, and implementing effective project management processes to ensure efficient and timely project delivery.
Water consumption management involves how we utilize water resources, whereas effluent management deals with the discharge of water and its ecological consequences.
Implementing a monitoring system for evaluating eco- efficiency and optimizing water usage through methods like robotic waterless cleaning, among others

With increasing awareness of ESG, environmental, social, and governance issues have become more important than ever to investors, customers, stakeholders, employees, communities, and regulators. Demonstrating strong ESG performance can enhance access to capital, attract top talent, and unlock new business opportunities.

The Company adopts an innovative approach to maximize the efficiency of its solar and hybrid assets through a state-of-the-art Centralized Monitoring System (CMS) and a dedicated Network Operations Centre (NOC). These platforms enable real-time monitoring, analysis, and swift resolution of issues, ensuring enhanced asset performance. By leveraging predictive maintenance, 24/7 operations oversight, automated reporting for informed decision-making, and continuous improvement through data-driven insights, the Company optimizes resource utilization and strengthens operational reliability.

The Company’s revenue streams are closely linked to capital expenditures in the solar and hybrid energy sector, primarily driven by Independent Power Producers (IPPs) and Captive Power Producers (CPPs). Consequently, fluctuations in the capital expenditure cycle—such as reduced investments or delayed decision-making by IPPs or CPPs—could adversely impact the Company’s financial performance.

By strengthening customer relationships, building long-term partnerships, and delivering value-added services, the Company aims to foster customer loyalty and maintain a stable and diversified customer base.

KPI Green Energy Limited contributes to the Group’s strategic commitment to achieve 10 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030, reflecting the Group’s dedication to sustainable growth and supporting India’s energy transition. The Company has integrated sustainability into its strategy, operations, and decision-making processes, with initiatives aimed at improving operational efficiency, reducing environmental impact, and ensuring workplace safety. Advanced technological solutions, such as real-time monitoring of assets and water-less robotic solar panel cleaning, are implemented to maintain optimal performance. Strengthened governance practices and structured stakeholder engagement ensure that KPI Green Energy’s activities support the Group’s long-term renewable energy and sustainability objectives in a measurable and time-bound manner.

KPI Green Energy monitors its contribution toward the Group’s 10 GW renewable energy target by 2030 through key KPIs, including installed capacity, operational efficiency, and safety. As of March 31, 2025, the Company’s portfolio, including orders in hand, stands at over 3.90 GW, forming a major part of the Group’s total portfolio of 5.75 GW. This highlights KPI Green Energy’s significant role in driving progress toward the Group’s long-term goal. Any deviations from interim milestones are analysed, and corrective measures such as process improvements and adoption of innovative technologies are implemented to ensure continued alignment and steady advancement toward the target.

At the Company, we remain committed to embedding sustainability into every aspect of our business, recognizing that our long-term success depends on balancing environmental stewardship, social responsibility, and governance excellence. While we continue to navigate challenges such as regulatory dynamics, climate-related risks, and community integration in project areas, our focus is on expanding solar and hybrid renewable energy capacity, reducing our carbon footprint, strengthening community engagement, and enhancing compliance and transparency. During the year, we have successfully commissioned solar and hybrid power projects contributing to clean energy generation, advanced livelihood and welfare initiatives in local communities, and reinforced governance systems through robust compliance practices. For us, ESG is not merely a reporting requirement but a strategic driver of resilience, trust, and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.

The Risk Management Committee of the Board is responsible for assisting the Board of Directors in fulfilling its responsibilities to oversee the Company’s significant strategies, policies and programs on social and public responsibility matters and for sustainability aspects with respect to the Company. 

Further, Dr. Alok Das, Group Chief Executive Officer (CEO) oversees the strategic and operational aspects of climate-related issues, sustainability performance and value creation.

The Risk Management Committee of the Board is responsible for decision making on sustainability related issues of the Company. Further details on the Risk Management Committee, including its composition and terms of reference, are provided in the Report on Corporate Governance forming part of this Annual Report.

The company follows a robust Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery policy committed to transparency and responsibility in all its actions, the Company upholds legal and ethical standards. It firmly opposes any bribery, embezzlement, or corruption and abides by laws against such conduct. The policy ensures that employees maintain the highest levels of honesty, integrity, and fairness, while performing their duties with sincerity and care. It also ensures that the Company equips its employees with effective systems to uphold the best standards of ethical conduct. The same can be accessed at https://www.kpigreenenergy.com/policies-disclosures.html

The Company is committed to promote an inclusive workplace that respects and supports the rights of all individuals, including persons with disabilities and strongly stands against discrimination based on gender, caste, religion, disability, or sex.

A grievance redressal mechanism is available for permanent employees. The system is designed to redress the grievance within a defined timeline of 15 working days. The grievances are resolved in fair and time bound manner maintaining utmost confidentiality.

The Company endeavours to prevent all injuries and work-related illnesses and is committed to provide safe and healthy working environment. The Company provides first aid training to its employees at all sites and offices to ensure preparedness in handling workplace emergencies

The Company conducts initial and periodic workplace inspections at all sites and offices place to identify the new or recurring hazards. This involves a thorough analysis of various factors, including tasks, responsibilities, potential risks and operational requirements

The company places a strong emphasis on prioritizing the safety of its workforce and has introduced various initiatives to reduce workplace injuries and promote safety awareness. To achieve this, the company conducts training programs that focus on employee well-being. Furthermore, the company has formulated an Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) policy, accessible through its official website at- https://www.kpigreenenergy.com/policies-disclosures.html.

The internal team of Company conducts regular audits, reviews and Safety-related accidents are thoroughly investigated. The findings from these investigations are used to prevent the recurrence of similar incidents. The effectiveness of corrective actions is evaluated during safety audits conducted. Measures are taken by the company like Health and Training sessions, first aid trainings, mock drills to ensure preparedness in handling workplace emergencies.

The Company identifies its key stakeholder groups through ongoing stakeholder engagement process, which includes customers, suppliers, employees, communities, government regulators and shareholders. This process allows the Company to recognise and respond stakeholders effectively. Engagement is based on regular transparent communication and a commitment to creating shared value. To support this, the Company has a Stakeholder Relationship Committee responsible for monitoring stakeholder engagement, addressing grievances, particularly those of shareholders and investors and ensuring transparent and timely communication.

The Company has internal grievance redressal mechanism handled by Human Resources Department and the Internal Compliance Team to address human rights-related concerns. These mechanisms ensure confidentiality, fair investigation and timely resolution of complaints raised by employees or stakeholders.

The Company is dedicated to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. It promotes equal opportunity for all individuals irrespective of gender, age, race, religion, caste, community, physical ability or gender identity through a strong, non-discriminatory policy framework. By creating a safe, respectful and enabling work environment, the Company empowers employees to perform at their best. Further, the Company is committed to maintaining a safe, inclusive and harassment-free workplace through a robust Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) Policy, applicable to all group companies. A duly constituted Internal Complaints Committee (ICC), functions in line with the POSH Act, who addresses complaints confidentially and fairly. The policy outlines a clear redressal and enquiry process, ensuring non-retaliation and protection of complainants. Disciplinary action is taken against proven cases, including termination if necessary.

Not Applicable, as a renewable energy generation and project development company, KPI Green Energy’s operations do not involve any processes that result in liquid discharge. Consequently, there is no impact on water resources or the environment in this regard, and the Company is not subject to regulations or permits related to liquid waste management. 

In solar power projects, limited water is used primarily for module cleaning and domestic purposes at project sites. With the adoption of robotic and dry-cleaning technologies, the Company has significantly reduced water consumption, ensuring efficient operations while conserving natural resources.

Yes, as a renewable energy company, the company’s core operations are inherently aligned with efforts to reduce Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions. By generating clean energy through solar and other renewable sources, the company plays a direct role in displacing conventional fossil fuel-based power generation, thereby contributing meaningfully to climate change mitigation.
The operations of the company are primarily in renewable power generation through solar and hybrid projects, which do not involve the use of hazardous or toxic chemicals in our core processes.

The Company has established a comprehensive mechanism to receive and redress grievances from the local community, ensuring continuous engagement and transparent communication. Grievances can be raised through various channels such as dedicated CSR initiatives and interventions, regular community meetings, awareness programs and through the official KPI’s website. These channels not only facilitate the collection of feedback and concerns but also serve as platforms to strengthen relationships with the communities. Internally, a dedicated team is responsible for addressing these grievances, ensuring timely resolution and follow-up. This team monitors the social impact of company operations and CSR activities and works proactively to understand community needs, address issues and implement development-focused solutions. This mechanism is ongoing and operates on a need basis, reflecting the company’s commitment to inclusive development, well-being, and sustained dialogue with the communities surrounding its operations.

The Company places high importance on customer satisfaction and values feedback as a key driver of service improvement. Ensuring the timely and effective resolution of concerns and complaints raised by stakeholders remains a core priority. All feedback is periodically reviewed, necessary actions are taken to address issues and improve processes. The Company remains committed to deliver a positive customer experience while adhering to applicable regulatory requirements.
